dictionaryVocabulary.description: The neutral first-person pronoun and the safest beginner default for 'I'.

example.kana: わたしはがくせいです。

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A casual first-person pronoun often used by men and boys.
A second-person pronoun. Use it sparingly; names are often more natural.
A standalone demonstrative for a thing near the speaker.
Used directly before a noun for something near the speaker.

example.japanese: この本はおもしろいです。
example.kana: このほんはおもしろいです。

dictionaryVocabulary.description: The everyday word for a book.

example.japanese: この本はおもしろいです。
example.kana: このほんはおもしろいです。

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A common writing tool, written in katakana.

exercises.title: Personal Pronouns
exercises.intro: Japanese has several words for 'I' depending on register. 'You' is used sparingly — it's more natural to say the person's name + さん instead. Third-person pronouns (he / she) also carry the meaning of 'boyfriend / girlfriend' in casual speech.
exercises.takeaway: Pick your 'I' by register. Skip 'you' — call the person by name + さん.

exercises.prompt: Say 'I am a student' using the neutral pronoun:
exercises.speakText: わたしは がくせいです。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: かのじょは せんせいです。

exercises.title: Demonstratives — Things (-れ)
exercises.intro: Use the -れ forms when you point at a thing on its own, without naming it. これ / それ / あれ / どれ stand alone as 'this / that / that over there / which'. They never attach to another noun.
exercises.takeaway: -れ = 'this/that/which THING' — standalone, no noun after it.

exercises.prompt: Which word means 'this (thing, near me)' — standalone, no noun after?
Ask 'What is this?' using the near-speaker standalone demonstrative:
exercises.speakText: これは なんですか。

exercises.prompt: Now answer that question — 'This is a pen.':

slot.hint: A common writing tool — written in katakana in Japanese.

exercises.speakText: これは ペンです。

exercises.title: Demonstratives — + Noun (-の)
exercises.intro: Use the -の forms when you place 'this/that/which' **directly in front of a noun**. 'This book' is この ほん — not これ ほん. Same spatial logic as -れ: こ = near me, そ = near you, あ = over there, ど = question.
exercises.takeaway: -の goes *before* a noun. この ほん = 'this book'.

exercises.prompt: Which form goes BEFORE a noun, as in 'this book'?
Say 'This book is interesting' — place the demonstrative before the noun:
patternJp: ほんは おもしろいです。

slot.hint: The 'near me + noun' form — not the standalone one.

exercises.speakText: この ほんは おもしろいです。

exercises.title: Demonstratives — Places (-こ)
exercises.intro: Use the -こ forms when you talk about a **place**. ここ / そこ / あそこ / どこ mean 'here / there / over there / where'. Note: 'over there' becomes あそこ (not あこ) — irregular, just memorise.
exercises.takeaway: -こ = a PLACE. Remember the irregular あそこ for 'over there'.

exercises.prompt: Which word means 'here' — a PLACE near me?
Say 'This is my house' using the place form:
patternJp: は わたしの いえです。
exercises.speakText: ここは わたしの いえです。

exercises.title: Demonstratives — Direction & Polite (-ちら)
exercises.intro: Use the -ちら forms for a **direction** ('this way / that way') or as a polite stand-in for a person or place. どちらさまですか — 'Who is this?' — is the polite way to ask someone's name on the phone or at the door.
exercises.takeaway: -ちら = polite or directional. どちらさまですか is the polite 'who are you?'.

exercises.prompt: On the phone, which word politely means 'who' — as in 'Who is this?'
Politely introduce Mr Yamada — 'This is Mr Yamada':
patternJp: は やまださんです。
exercises.speakText: こちらは やまださんです。

exercises.prompt: Which correctly says 'This is a book.'?
exercises.title: これ vs この — standalone vs pre-noun

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.note: これ stands alone as 'this thing', followed by は + predicate.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.note: この attaches to a noun but the sentence has no topic marker は.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: これ ほんは おもしろいです。
variants.note: Can't put これ directly before a noun — use この for 'this book'.

variants.label: DIFFERENT MEANING
variants.note: Grammatical but says 'that over there', not 'this'.

exercises.takeaway: これ stands alone. この needs a noun attached. Don't mix them.
exercises.speakText: これは ほんです。

exercises.prompt: You're introducing yourself at a meeting. Which sounds most natural?
exercises.title: Drop pronouns when context is clear

variants.label: NATURAL
variants.note: Context makes 'I' obvious — Japanese drops pronouns when clear.

variants.label: UNNATURAL
variants.jp: わたしは がくせいです、わたしは にほんじんです。
variants.romaji: watashi wa gakusei desu, watashi wa nihonjin desu.
variants.en: I am a student, I am Japanese.
variants.note: Repeating わたし sentence after sentence sounds robotic.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: ぼく がくせいです。
variants.note: Missing the topic particle は — should be ぼくは.

exercises.takeaway: Drop pronouns when context is clear. And don't drop the particle は.
exercises.speakText: がくせいです。

exercises.prompt: You're talking to Tanaka. Which is the polite way to ask 'Are you a teacher?'
exercises.title: あなた warning — usually rude in Japanese

variants.label: POLITE
variants.jp: たなかさんは せんせいですか。
variants.note: Use the person's name + さん instead of あなた.

variants.label: RUDE
variants.jp: あなたは せんせいですか。
variants.note: あなた can sound confrontational — avoid with people you know.

variants.label: RUDE
variants.jp: きみは せんせいですか。
variants.note: きみ talks down to the listener — only between close friends or from a senior.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: あなたさんは せんせいですか。
variants.note: You cannot attach さん to あなた — さん goes on names.

exercises.takeaway: Skip あなた and きみ. Use the person's name + さん.
exercises.speakText: たなかさんは せんせいですか。

exercises.prompt: Which correctly says 'We are students.'?
exercises.title: ～たち for plural pronouns

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: わたしたちは がくせいです。
variants.note: Attach ～たち to a singular pronoun to make it plural.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: かれらたちは がくせいです。
variants.note: かれら already means 'they' — don't stack ～たち on top.

variants.label: WRONG
variants.jp: わたしは がくせいたちです。
variants.note: ～たち attaches to the SPEAKER pronoun, not to the predicate noun.

exercises.takeaway: Attach ～たち to the pronoun. Don't double-pluralise かれら.
exercises.speakText: わたしたちは がくせいです。

exercises.title: Questions & Answers in Context
exercises.intro: Never learn a question without its answer — you'll freeze when it's asked back to you. Here are the core question forms from this lesson paired with natural replies. Tap the speaker next to each row to hear it.
rows: わたしは がくせいです。
これは なんですか。
あの ひとは だれですか。
ano hito wa dare desu ka.
Q: Who is that person over there?
あの ひとは やまださんです。
ここは わたしの いえです。
koko wa watashi no ie desu.
わたしは すずきと もうします。
exercises.takeaway: Every question has a canonical answer — learn them together.

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: これは なんですか。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: これは ペンです。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: あの ひとは だれですか。
options: Who is that person over there?

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: あの ひとは やまださんです。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: わたしは がくせいです。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: ここは わたしの いえです。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: どちらさまですか。

exercises.prompt: Listen and choose the correct English meaning:
exercises.speakText: わたしは すずきと もうします。

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: わたしは がくせいです

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji (Q):
exercises.speakText: これは なんですか

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji (A):
exercises.speakText: これは ペンです

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji (Q):
exercises.speakText: あの ひとは だれですか
exercises.answer: ano hito wa dare desu ka

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji (A):
exercises.speakText: あの ひとは やまださんです
acceptAlternatives: ano hito wa yamada san desu

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: ここは わたしの いえです
exercises.answer: koko wa watashi no ie desu

exercises.prompt: Listen and type what you hear in romaji:
exercises.speakText: どちらさまですか

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its meaning:

cards.jp: これは なんですか。
あの ひとは だれですか。
cards.romaji: ano hito wa dare desu ka.
cards.en: Q: Who is that person over there?

cards.jp: あの ひとは やまださんです。
わたしは すずきと もうします。
どこに すんでいますか。
とうきょうに すんでいます。
ここは わたしの いえです。
cards.romaji: koko wa watashi no ie desu.

cards.jp: この ほんは おもしろいです。
